mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that sustains corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation confirms healthy economic expansion without overheating, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect stronger corporate revenue fundamentals that translate into immediate positive market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations and generates negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a liquidity trap environment reflects severe demand destruction that triggers immediate risk aversion and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.
